secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with heavy regulatory/banking domain

level_signals: Pay Grade - P / Equity Grade - 7 signals Staff-level compensation band
Single-threaded ownership of an entire bank charter buildout
Communicate strategy to senior leadership and Bank Board

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of product management experience, with demonstrated ability to operate as a strategic, independent leader on complex, ambiguous initiatives
Proven program leadership at scale — you've owned high visibility cross-functional launches across many stakeholders, with direct exposure to executive leadership and/or boards.
Experience in banking, deposits, or lending — you understand deposit products, bank charter operations, or loan origination mechanics and the regulatory landscape that governs them
Strong technical fluency — you're comfortable working with engineers on system design, data models, API architecture, and core banking platform integrations
Proficiency with AI-assisted tools (e.g., Claude, Cursor) to accelerate your own work and raise the bar for quality and speed
Demonstrated ability to drive cross-functional alignment across Legal, Compliance, Engineering, Finance, and executive leadership in highly regulated environments

Exceptional written and verbal communication — you can distill complex regulatory and financial concepts into clear, compelling narratives for diverse audiences including regulators, executives, and engineering teams
Bias for action and intellectual curiosity — you move fast, ask hard questions, and take ownership before being asked
Bachelor's degree in a related field or equivalent practical experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bank charter experience is strongly preferred — you've been through or contributed meaningfully to a de novo bank launch or early-stage bank buildout

Deposit product strategy experience is a strong plus — you've defined or scaled deposit products such as high yield savings accounts, spending accounts, or CDs, and you understand the levers of deposit growth, pricing, and consumer acquisition
Experience in fintech, financial services, or banking; familiarity with BNPL, neobanking, or platform products is a plus

scope.surface: Affirm Bank — deposit product, loan originations, bank charter operationalization, and integration with Affirm's consumer platform
scope.business_model: B2C consumer banking and BNPL, de novo bank charter, deposit-funded lending
primary_metrics_inferred: Deposit base growth (target: multi-billion-dollar deposit base)
Loan origination volume through bank entity
Unit economics of loan retention and sale mechanics
core_responsibilities: Own the end-to-end bank product across deposits, loan originations, banking compliance, and operations
Launch and own the bank's deposit product and roadmap
Operationalize the de novo bank build including regulatory readiness, process maturity, and vendor integrations
Drive originations through the bank entity working with Credit, Capital Markets, Engineering, and Treasury
Navigate banking compliance and regulatory requirements with Legal, Compliance, Risk, and Bank executive team
Integrate the bank deposit product into Affirm's consumer ecosystem across multiple platform teams
Communicate strategy, tradeoffs, and priorities to senior leadership and the Bank Board

compensation.equity_form: Equity Grade 7; ESPP (employee stock purchase plan with discount); equity rewards mentioned as part of total comp
compensation.notes: CAN base pay range per year: $181,000 - $241,000 (Canadian dollars, not USD). No USD base range stated.

green_flags: Rare, high-impact opportunity to build a foundational bank from scratch
Single-threaded ownership with executive and Bank Board exposure
Remote-first company with wide US geographic eligibility
Compensation package includes 100% subsidized medical coverage for dependents
Clearly scoped role with specific deliverables (deposit product launch, loan originations, bank charter operationalization)
Pay grade and equity grade disclosed (P / Grade 7), aiding level comparison
red_flags: No USD base pay range stated — only Canadian dollar range ($181K-$241K CAD) provided, making US compensation opaque
Bank charter experience is 'strongly preferred' but classified as nice-to-have; may function as de facto hard filter given role complexity
Extremely broad scope (deposits + originations + compliance + platform integration) could indicate under-resourcing or unclear team boundaries
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in posting

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from Pay Grade P, Equity Grade 7, and scope signals)

ambiguities: Bank charter experience is described as 'strongly preferred' (suggesting nice-to-have) but the role is literally to build a de novo bank, making it functionally a hard requirement in practice. Classified as nice-to-have per JD language but flagged here.

The compensation range ($181,000–$241,000) is explicitly labeled 'CAN base pay range' (Canadian dollars). No US base pay range is provided despite the role being US remote-first. This is ambiguous and potentially confusing.
JD hashtag is #LI-Remote and text confirms remote-first; no office requirement stated for this role, so treated as remote-us.
